---
title: "Plot_sampling"
author: "Wouter Strauven"
date: "2026-06-25"
output: html_document
---

0. Setup
```{r setup}
# Set knit root to the project folder so relative paths like Output/... resolve in VS Code.
find_project_root <- function(start = getwd()) {
  current <- normalizePath(start, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
  repeat {
    if (file.exists(file.path(current, "R.Rproj"))) return(current)
    parent <- dirname(current)
    if (identical(parent, current)) return(NULL)
    current <- parent
  }
}

project_root <- find_project_root()
if (!is.null(project_root)) {
  knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)
}

# Libraries
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rlang)
library(stringr)

```

1. Read in layers
```{r}
# Study area
Study_area <- vect("F:/KULeuven/BRANCH/Materials_and_methods/Sampling_design/Study_Area/BRANCH_StudyArea_31370.gpkg")

# Read in stratification layers
LCZ <- rast("Output/LCZ_3classes.tif")
OSM <- rast("Output/OSM_raster_10m.tif")

# Inspect
Study_area
LCZ
OSM
crs(Study_area)
crs(LCZ)
crs(OSM)

# Fix crs
crs(OSM) <- "EPSG:31370"
OSM <- project(OSM, LCZ)

# Provide unique names for columns
names(LCZ) <- "LCZ_class"
names(OSM) <- "OSM_class"

```

2. Stratified sampling
2.1. Define functions
```{r}
# Create a square polygon (SpatVector) centered on a point (x,y) with side 'size' (meters)
make_square <- function(x, y, size, crs) {
  half <- size / 2
  coords <- matrix(
    c(x - half, y - half,
      x + half, y - half,
      x + half, y + half,
      x - half, y + half,
      x - half, y - half),
    ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  p <- vect(list(coords), type = "polygons", crs = crs)
  p
}

# Sample candidate points inside a polygon but at least 'half_size' away from boundary
sample_candidates_interior <- function(poly, n_candidates = 1000, half_size = 50, seed = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  # Erode polygon by half_size to ensure square fits inside
  inner <- try(buffer(poly, width = -half_size), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(inner, "try-error") || nrow(inner) == 0) {
    # If erosion removes polygon (too small), fall back to original polygon but warn
    inner <- poly
  }
  # Use spatSample to draw random points inside inner polygon
  pts <- spatSample(inner, size = n_candidates, method = "random", as.points = TRUE)
  # If spatSample returns fewer than requested, return what we have
  pts
}

# Minimal precondition check (put at start of function)
if (!identical(terra::crs(Study_area), terra::crs(LCZ))) {
  stop("CRS mismatch: Study_area and LCZ must share the same CRS before calling this function.")
}
if (!identical(terra::crs(LCZ), terra::crs(OSM))) {
  stop("CRS mismatch: LCZ and OSM must share the same CRS before calling this function.")
}

# Compute percent cover of raster classes inside a polygon
percent_cover_in_polygon <- function(rast, poly) {
  # crop and mask
  r_crop <- crop(rast, poly, snap = "out")
  r_mask <- mask(r_crop, poly)
  vals <- values(r_mask)
  vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
  if (length(vals) == 0) return(tibble(class = NA_integer_, count = 0L, pct = 0))
  tab <- as.data.frame(table(vals), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(tab) <- c("class", "count")
  tab <- tab %>% mutate(class = as.integer(as.character(class)))
  tab <- tab %>% mutate(pct = 100 * count / sum(count))
  tab
}

# Main function: sample plots per city
sample_plots_per_city <- function(
  Study_area,        # SpatVector with city polygons (one feature per city)
  LCZ,               # raster with LCZ_class
  OSM,               # raster with OSM_class
  city_id_field = "fid",   # field in Study_area identifying cities (adjust if different)
  cities = NULL,     # vector of city IDs to process (if NULL -> all)
  n_plots_city = 20,
  plot_size = 100,   # meters (square side)
  seed = 1234,
  use_strata = TRUE,
  strata_vars = c("LCZ_class", "OSM_class"), # which raster attributes to use for stratification
  strata_mode = c("proportional", "equal"),
  n_candidates = 2000,   # number of candidate points to draw per city (must be >= n_plots_city)
  allowed_osm = NULL,    # optional vector of OSM_class values to include (e.g., c(1,2,3,4))
  export_gpkg = FALSE,
  export_folder = "exports",
  export_name_prefix = "plots"
) {
  strata_mode <- match.arg(strata_mode)
  set.seed(seed)
  # checks
  if (!inherits(Study_area, "SpatVector")) stop("Study_area must be a SpatVector")
  if (!inherits(LCZ, "SpatRaster") || !inherits(OSM, "SpatRaster")) stop("LCZ and OSM must be SpatRaster")
  if (!is.null(cities)) {
    Study_area <- Study_area[Study_area[[city_id_field]] %in% cities, ]
  }

  # prepare outputs (lazy init avoids terra::vect(crs=...) incompatibility)
  all_points <- NULL
  all_squares <- NULL
  plot_stats <- list()
  # iterate cities
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(Study_area))) {
    city_poly <- Study_area[i, ]
    city_id <- city_poly[[city_id_field]]
    message("Sampling city: ", city_id)
    # sample candidate points inside eroded polygon
    half_size <- plot_size / 2
    candidates <- sample_candidates_interior(city_poly, n_candidates = n_candidates, half_size = half_size, seed = seed + i)
    if (is.null(candidates) || nrow(candidates) == 0) {
      warning("No candidate points for city ", city_id, " (polygon too small after erosion). Skipping.")
      next
    }
    # extract LCZ and OSM at candidate points
    cand_df <- as.data.frame(candidates, geom = "XY")
    cand_df$ID_pt <- seq_len(nrow(cand_df))
    # extract raster values
    cand_df$LCZ_class <- terra::extract(LCZ, candidates)[, "LCZ_class"]
    cand_df$OSM_class <- terra::extract(OSM, candidates)[, "OSM_class"]
    # optionally filter allowed OSM classes (e.g., exclude 'Overige')
    if (!is.null(allowed_osm)) {
      cand_df <- cand_df %>% filter(OSM_class %in% allowed_osm)
    }
    # remove NA strata candidates
    cand_df <- cand_df %>% filter(!is.na(LCZ_class) | !is.na(OSM_class))
    if (nrow(cand_df) == 0) {
      warning("No valid candidate points with strata values for city ", city_id)
      next
    }
    # Decide sampling strategy: stratify by each strata_var separately (user requested separate stratification)
    # We'll perform sequential stratified sampling: first ensure LCZ distribution (if present), then OSM on remaining slots.
    n_needed <- n_plots_city
    selected_ids <- integer(0)
    # Helper to sample by one strata variable
    sample_by_stratum <- function(df, var, n_slots, mode) {
      if (n_slots <= 0) return(integer(0))
      df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))
      if (nrow(df) == 0) return(integer(0))
      if (mode == "proportional") {
        counts <- df %>% count(!!sym(var), name = "n_stratum")
        counts <- counts %>% mutate(alloc = pmax(1, round(n_stratum / sum(n_stratum) * n_slots)))
      } else { # equal
        n_strata <- n_distinct(df[[var]])
        per_stratum <- ceiling(n_slots / n_strata)
        counts <- df %>% distinct(!!sym(var)) %>% mutate(alloc = per_stratum)
        names(counts)[1] <- var
      }
      df <- df %>% left_join(counts %>% select(!!sym(var), alloc), by = var)
      picks <- df %>% group_by(!!sym(var)) %>%
        group_modify(~ {
          n_alloc <- unique(.x$alloc)
          n_alloc <- ifelse(length(n_alloc) == 0, 0, n_alloc[1])
          if (n_alloc <= 0) return(.x[0, ])
          n_draw <- min(n_alloc, nrow(.x))
          .x %>% slice_sample(n = n_draw)
        }) %>% ungroup()
      picks$ID_pt
    }
    # 1) stratify by LCZ_class if requested
    if (use_strata && "LCZ_class" %in% strata_vars) {
      ids_lcz <- sample_by_stratum(cand_df %>% filter(!(ID_pt %in% selected_ids)), "LCZ_class", n_needed, strata_mode)
      selected_ids <- c(selected_ids, ids_lcz)
      n_needed <- n_plots_city - length(selected_ids)
    }
    # 2) stratify by OSM_class if requested (fill remaining)
    if (n_needed > 0 && use_strata && "OSM_class" %in% strata_vars) {
      ids_osm <- sample_by_stratum(cand_df %>% filter(!(ID_pt %in% selected_ids)), "OSM_class", n_needed, strata_mode)
      selected_ids <- c(selected_ids, ids_osm)
      n_needed <- n_plots_city - length(selected_ids)
    }
    # 3) if still short, fill randomly from remaining candidates
    if (n_needed > 0) {
      remaining <- cand_df %>% filter(!(ID_pt %in% selected_ids))
      if (nrow(remaining) > 0) {
        extra <- remaining %>% slice_sample(n = min(n_needed, nrow(remaining)))
        selected_ids <- c(selected_ids, extra$ID_pt)
      }
    }
    # Build selected points and squares
    sel_df <- cand_df %>% filter(ID_pt %in% selected_ids)
    if (nrow(sel_df) == 0) {
      warning("No selected points for city ", city_id)
      next
    }
    pts_sel <- vect(sel_df, geom = c("x", "y"), crs = crs(Study_area))
    # create squares
    squares_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sel_df)), function(j) {
      make_square(sel_df$x[j], sel_df$y[j], plot_size, crs = crs(Study_area))
    })
    squares <- do.call(rbind, squares_list)
    # attach attributes
    pts_sel$city_id <- rep(city_id, nrow(pts_sel))
    pts_sel$city_name <- rep(if ("name" %in% names(city_poly)) city_poly$name else as.character(city_id), nrow(pts_sel))
    pts_sel$LCZ_center <- sel_df$LCZ_class
    pts_sel$OSM_center <- sel_df$OSM_class
    pts_sel$ID_plot <- paste0(as.character(city_id), "_", seq_len(nrow(pts_sel)))
    squares$ID_plot <- pts_sel$ID_plot
    # compute percent cover for each square (LCZ and OSM)
    stats_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(squares)), function(j) {
      sq <- squares[j, ]
      lcz_tab <- percent_cover_in_polygon(LCZ, sq)
      osm_tab <- percent_cover_in_polygon(OSM, sq)
      tibble(
        ID_plot = squares$ID_plot[j],
        city_id = city_id,
        x = sel_df$x[j],
        y = sel_df$y[j],
        LCZ_center = sel_df$LCZ_class[j],
        OSM_center = sel_df$OSM_class[j],
        LCZ_cover = list(lcz_tab),
        OSM_cover = list(osm_tab)
      )
    })
    stats_df <- bind_rows(stats_list)
    # collect outputs
    all_points <- if (is.null(all_points)) pts_sel else rbind(all_points, pts_sel)
    all_squares <- if (is.null(all_squares)) squares else rbind(all_squares, squares)
    plot_stats[[as.character(city_id)]] <- stats_df
  } # end city loop

  if (is.null(all_points) || is.null(all_squares)) {
    warning("No plots were generated. Check selected cities and candidate settings.")
    return(list(
      points = NULL,
      squares = NULL,
      stats = tibble()
    ))
  }

  # combine stats
  stats_combined <- bind_rows(plot_stats, .id = "city_id_list") %>%
    select(-city_id_list)

  # optional export
  if (export_gpkg && !is.null(all_points) && !is.null(all_squares)) {
    if (!dir.exists(export_folder)) dir.create(export_folder)
    gpkg_pts <- file.path(export_folder, paste0(export_name_prefix, "_points.gpkg"))
    gpkg_sq  <- file.path(export_folder, paste0(export_name_prefix, "_squares.gpkg"))
    writeVector(all_points, gpkg_pts, overwrite = TRUE)
    writeVector(all_squares, gpkg_sq, overwrite = TRUE)
  }

  list(
    points = all_points,
    squares = all_squares,
    stats = stats_combined
  )
}
```

2.2 Run functions
```{r}


# Select desired provinces
# fid list:
# 1. Brussel; 2. Luik /Liège; 3. Aarlen/Arlon; 4. Leuven; 5. Namen/Namur; 6. Bergen/Mons; 7. Gent; 8. Brugge; 9. Hasselt; 10. Waver/Wavre; 11. Antwerpen; 12. Oostende
Provinces <- c(1, 2, 4, 9)
Study_area_sub <- Study_area[Study_area$fid %in% Provinces, ]

res_plots <- sample_plots_per_city(
  Study_area = Study_area_sub,
  LCZ = LCZ,
  OSM = OSM,
  city_id_field = "fid",
  cities = NULL,
  n_plots_city = 20,
  plot_size = 100,
  seed = 42,
  use_strata = TRUE,
  strata_vars = c("LCZ_class", "OSM_class"),
  strata_mode = "proportional",
  n_candidates = 2000,
  allowed_osm = NULL,
  export_gpkg = TRUE,
  export_folder = "exports",
  export_name_prefix = "veg_plots"
)

# outputs
res_plots$points     # SpatVector of center points with attributes
res_plots$squares    # SpatVector of 100x100 polygons
res_plots$stats      # tibble with LCZ/OSM center and percent-cover tables per plot

```







Helper functions
```{r}
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

#--------------------------------------------------
# Select cities
#--------------------------------------------------
Study_area$city <- toupper(
  dplyr::coalesce(
    as.character(Study_area$mun_name_1_2),
    as.character(Study_area$mun_name_1),
    as.character(Study_area$mun_name_f),
    as.character(Study_area$mun_name_n)
  )
)
Study_area$city[Study_area$city %in% c(
  "WATERMAAL-BOSVOORDE",
  "WATERMAEL-BOITSFORT",
  "BRUSSEL",
  "BRUXELLES"
)] <- "BRUSSEL"

selected_cities <- c("BRUSSEL", "LUIK", "LEUVEN", "HASSELT")

Study_area_sub <- Study_area[
  Study_area$city %in% selected_cities,
]

#--------------------------------------------------
# Create 100x100 m square around point
#--------------------------------------------------
make_square <- function(x, y, size = 100) {

  half <- size / 2

  coords <- matrix(
    c(
      x-half, y-half,
      x+half, y-half,
      x+half, y+half,
      x-half, y+half,
      x-half, y-half
    ),
    ncol = 2,
    byrow = TRUE
  )

  vect(
    list(coords),
    type = "polygons"
  )
}

#--------------------------------------------------
# Cover percentages
#--------------------------------------------------
cover_percentages <- function(r, poly, prefix) {

  vals <- values(
    mask(
      crop(r, poly),
      poly
    )
  )

  vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]

  if(length(vals) == 0) {
    return(data.frame())
  }

  out <- prop.table(table(vals)) * 100

  out <- data.frame(
    class = names(out),
    pct = as.numeric(out)
  )

  names(out)[2] <- "pct"

  out |>
    mutate(var = paste0(prefix, "_", class)) |>
    select(var, pct) |>
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = var,
      values_from = pct,
      values_fill = 0
    )
}
```


Balanced sampler
```{r}
sample_city_plots <- function(
    city_poly,
    LCZ,
    OSM,
    city_id,
    n_plots = 20,
    plot_size = 100,
    min_distance = 100,
    n_candidates = 10000,
    allowed_osm = NULL,
    seed = 42
) {

  set.seed(seed)

  half_size <- plot_size / 2

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Shrink city boundary
  #--------------------------------------------------

  city_inner <- buffer(city_poly, -half_size)

  if(nrow(city_inner) == 0) {
    warning(paste("City", city_id, "too small"))
    return(NULL)
  }

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Candidate points
  #--------------------------------------------------

  pts <- spatSample(
    city_inner,
    size = n_candidates,
    method = "random"
  )

  cand <- as.data.frame(pts, geom = "XY")

  cand$id <- seq_len(nrow(cand))

  cand$LCZ <- terra::extract(LCZ, pts)[,2]
  cand$OSM <- terra::extract(OSM, pts)[,2]

  cand <- cand |>
    filter(
      !is.na(LCZ),
      !is.na(OSM)
    )

  if(!is.null(allowed_osm)) {
    cand <- cand |>
      filter(OSM %in% allowed_osm)
  }

  if(nrow(cand) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Target distributions
  #--------------------------------------------------

  lcz_classes <- sort(unique(cand$LCZ))
  osm_classes <- sort(unique(cand$OSM))

  target_lcz <- setNames(
    rep(n_plots / length(lcz_classes),
        length(lcz_classes)),
    lcz_classes
  )

  target_osm <- setNames(
    rep(n_plots / length(osm_classes),
        length(osm_classes)),
    osm_classes
  )

  selected <- integer()

  lcz_count <- setNames(
    rep(0, length(lcz_classes)),
    lcz_classes
  )

  osm_count <- setNames(
    rep(0, length(osm_classes)),
    osm_classes
  )

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Balanced greedy selection
  #--------------------------------------------------

  while(length(selected) < n_plots) {

    remaining <- cand |>
      filter(!(id %in% selected))

    if(nrow(remaining) == 0) break

    scores <- rep(0, nrow(remaining))

    for(i in seq_len(nrow(remaining))) {

      lcz <- as.character(remaining$LCZ[i])
      osm <- as.character(remaining$OSM[i])

      lcz_deficit <- target_lcz[lcz] - lcz_count[lcz]
      osm_deficit <- target_osm[osm] - osm_count[osm]

      scores[i] <- lcz_deficit + osm_deficit
    }

    remaining$score <- scores

    remaining <- remaining |>
      arrange(desc(score))

    chosen <- NULL

    for(i in seq_len(nrow(remaining))) {

      candidate <- remaining[i,]

      if(length(selected) == 0) {

        chosen <- candidate
        break

      }

      sel <- cand[cand$id %in% selected,]

      d <- sqrt(
        (sel$x - candidate$x)^2 +
        (sel$y - candidate$y)^2
      )

      if(min(d) >= min_distance) {

        chosen <- candidate
        break

      }
    }

    if(is.null(chosen)) break

    selected <- c(selected, chosen$id)

    lcz_count[as.character(chosen$LCZ)] <-
      lcz_count[as.character(chosen$LCZ)] + 1

    osm_count[as.character(chosen$OSM)] <-
      osm_count[as.character(chosen$OSM)] + 1
  }

  selected_df <- cand |>
    filter(id %in% selected)

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Points
  #--------------------------------------------------

  points <- vect(
    selected_df,
    geom = c("x","y"),
    crs = crs(city_poly)
  )

  points$city_id <- city_id

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Squares
  #--------------------------------------------------

  sq_list <- lapply(
    seq_len(nrow(selected_df)),
    function(i) {

      sq <- make_square(
        selected_df$x[i],
        selected_df$y[i],
        plot_size
      )

      crs(sq) <- crs(city_poly)

      sq
    }
  )

  squares <- do.call(rbind, sq_list)

  squares$plot_id <- paste0(
    city_id,
    "_",
    seq_len(nrow(squares))
  )

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # Plot statistics
  #--------------------------------------------------

  stats <- vector(
    "list",
    nrow(squares)
  )

  for(i in seq_len(nrow(squares))) {

    sq <- squares[i,]

    lcz_cov <- cover_percentages(
      LCZ,
      sq,
      "LCZ"
    )

    osm_cov <- cover_percentages(
      OSM,
      sq,
      "OSM"
    )

    stats[[i]] <- bind_cols(

      data.frame(

        plot_id = squares$plot_id[i],

        city_id = city_id,

        x = selected_df$x[i],

        y = selected_df$y[i],

        LCZ_center = selected_df$LCZ[i],

        OSM_center = selected_df$OSM[i]
      ),

      lcz_cov,
      osm_cov
    )
  }

  stats <- bind_rows(stats)

  list(
    points = points,
    squares = squares,
    stats = stats,
    lcz_distribution = lcz_count,
    osm_distribution = osm_count
  )
}
```

Run for all cities
```{r}
all_points <- NULL
all_squares <- NULL
all_stats <- list()

for(i in seq_len(nrow(Study_area_sub))) {

  city <- Study_area_sub[i,]

  city_id <- city$fid

  res <- sample_city_plots(

    city_poly = city,

    LCZ = LCZ,

    OSM = OSM,

    city_id = city_id,

    n_plots = 20,

    plot_size = 100,

    min_distance = 150,

    n_candidates = 10000,

    allowed_osm = c(1,2,3,4),

    seed = 42 + i
  )

  if(is.null(res)) next

  if(is.null(all_points)) {
    all_points <- res$points
  } else {
    all_points <- rbind(all_points, res$points)
  }

  if(is.null(all_squares)) {
    all_squares <- res$squares
  } else {
    all_squares <- rbind(all_squares, res$squares)
  }

  all_stats[[as.character(city_id)]] <- res$stats

  cat(
    "\nCity:",
    city_id,
    "\nLCZ:",
    paste(names(res$lcz_distribution),
          res$lcz_distribution,
          collapse = ", "),
    "\nOSM:",
    paste(names(res$osm_distribution),
          res$osm_distribution,
          collapse = ", "),
    "\n"
  )
}

all_stats <- bind_rows(all_stats)
```

Export
```{r}
if (!dir.exists("exports")) dir.create("exports")

if (!is.null(all_points)) {
  writeVector(
    all_points,
    "exports/veg_plot_points.gpkg",
    overwrite = TRUE
  )
} else {
  warning("No point plots to export.")
}

if (!is.null(all_squares)) {
  writeVector(
    all_squares,
    "exports/veg_plot_squares.gpkg",
    overwrite = TRUE
  )
} else {
  warning("No square plots to export.")
}

if (nrow(all_stats) > 0) {
  write.csv(
    all_stats,
    "exports/veg_plot_statistics.csv",
    row.names = FALSE
  )
} else {
  warning("No plot statistics to export.")
}
```
